step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("most common type of non-hodgkins lymphoma in eye")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

```plaintext
Based on the detailed evidence gathered, the predominant type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma affecting the orbit indeed aligns strongly with B-cell types, particularly MALT (Marginal Zone Lymphoma Type) subtypes. Multiple sources confirm that B-cell lymphomas represent approximately 90% of all orbital lymphomas, with MALT being the most frequently occurring variety. These statistics underscore why B-cell lymphomas stand out as the most common in terms of prevalence compared to others like T-cell or natural killer (NK)-cell variants. Therefore, I concur fully with my initial stance emphasizing the primacy of B-cell lymphomas in orbital settings given consistent empirical data reinforcing this pattern clinically and pathologically.
\boxed{A}
```
```plaintext
I reiterate agreement with Option A - B-cell lymphoma remains the most common type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma found in the orbit based on substantial corroborating evidence showing a clear preponderance of B-cell involvement especially via MALT subtyping. Given this overwhelmingly supportive documentation, no compelling reasons exist to deviate from prior conclusions affirming B-cell dominance here.
